sections.paragraph:
fut. יִנְהֹם-

(1) to growl (knurren, brummen); the word used to express the noise uttered by the young lion (כְּפִיר), ( Pro 19:12 20:2 ); to be distinguished from roaring (שָׁאַג), although this word is also applied to a full-grown lion, Pro 28:15 (This root is onomatop. Arab. and Syr. id.; see under הָמָה ; and also compare נָאַם). It is applied to the roaring of the sea, Isa 5:30 to the voices of persons groaning (compare הָמָה), Eze 24:23 Pro 5:11. Hence-
